description: Cross-agent handoff definitions for proactive operations. Agents read this at runtime from .handoff-protocol.json in their workspace.

handoffs.trigger: Kit resolves a complex or recurring bug
handoffs.payload: Bug description, root cause, fix approach, prevention strategy
handoffs.action: Scribe creates a knowledge article documenting the bug pattern

handoffs.trigger: Trak completes a sprint summary or retrospective
handoffs.payload: Sprint metrics, velocity, blockers, team feedback
handoffs.action: Scribe captures sprint insights in documentation

handoffs.trigger: Scout resolves a customer issue that reveals a common pattern
handoffs.payload: Issue description, resolution steps, frequency data
handoffs.action: Scribe creates/updates FAQ or troubleshooting guide

handoffs.trigger: Scout identifies repeated customer feature requests
handoffs.payload: Feature description, customer count, business impact estimate
handoffs.action: Trak creates a Jira feature request with customer evidence

handoffs.trigger: Kit identifies significant technical debt during code review
handoffs.payload: Debt description, affected components, estimated effort, risk level
handoffs.action: Trak creates a tech debt Jira issue with proper prioritization

handoffs.trigger: Trak detects a PR that's been open and idle for 48+ hours
handoffs.payload: PR number, author, reviewer status, blocking reason if known
handoffs.action: Kit pings reviewers and offers to help unblock

handoffs.trigger: Scribe detects documentation that's 90+ days stale and references active systems
handoffs.payload: Document link, last updated date, systems referenced, suggested owner
handoffs.action: Notify the relevant agent to review and update their domain docs

handoffs.trigger: Scout receives multiple customer reports about the same bug
handoffs.payload: Bug description, affected customers count, reproduction steps, ticket links
handoffs.action: Kit investigates the bug and creates a GitHub issue if confirmed

handoffs.trigger: A deployment completes (tag pushed, deploy hook fires)
handoffs.payload: Deploy tag, environment URL, list of merged PRs
handoffs.action: Probe runs post-deploy smoke tests against critical user flows

handoffs.trigger: A Jira bug ticket has reproduction steps and needs verification
handoffs.payload: Jira ticket ID, reproduction steps, environment details
handoffs.action: Probe attempts browser-based reproduction and attaches evidence to ticket

handoffs.trigger: Probe successfully reproduces a reported bug
handoffs.payload: Jira ticket ID, screenshots, console logs, exact reproduction steps
handoffs.action: Kit investigates the root cause and creates a fix

handoffs.trigger: Post-deploy smoke tests complete
handoffs.payload: Pass/fail summary, deploy tag, test execution report
handoffs.action: Trak updates the deployment tracking record with test results

handoffs.trigger: Probe identifies missing test specifications or undocumented flows
handoffs.payload: Missing test specs, undocumented user flows, coverage gaps
handoffs.action: Scribe creates documentation for the missing test areas

handoffs.trigger: Kit creates an auto-fix PR that needs tracking
handoffs.payload: PR number, fix type (lint, dependency, security), affected components
handoffs.action: Trak adds the auto-fix PR to sprint tracking and ensures review assignment

handoffs.trigger: Scribe's knowledge gap analysis identifies undocumented features
handoffs.payload: List of documentation gaps with severity, suggested owners, Jira task IDs
handoffs.action: Trak prioritizes documentation tasks within sprint planning

handoffs.trigger: Chief identifies an operational improvement that needs project tracking
handoffs.payload: Improvement title, current state, proposed change, expected impact, priority
handoffs.action: Trak creates a Jira improvement task with Chief's analysis

handoffs.trigger: Chief identifies high-cost customer support patterns from financial data
handoffs.payload: Cost breakdown, customer segment, support ticket volume, recommended changes
handoffs.action: Scout reviews support patterns and suggests process improvements

handoffs.trigger: Chief detects infrastructure costs that could be optimized
handoffs.payload: Service costs, usage patterns, optimization recommendations
handoffs.action: Kit evaluates technical feasibility of cost optimization

handoffs.trigger: Chief completes a financial or operational analysis
handoffs.payload: Analysis summary, key metrics, recommendations, supporting data
handoffs.action: Scribe documents the analysis in Notion for stakeholder review

handoffs.trigger: Trak detects resource allocation issues from sprint data
handoffs.payload: Sprint metrics, team velocity, bottleneck areas, allocation data
handoffs.action: Chief analyzes resource utilization and proposes rebalancing

handoffs.trigger: Scout identifies a surge in support costs or ticket volume
handoffs.payload: Ticket volume trends, resolution times, cost per ticket, category breakdown
handoffs.action: Chief correlates with financial data and assesses business impact

handoffs.trigger: Kit completes a deploy that has potential financial impact
handoffs.payload: Deploy tag, changes summary, affected services, estimated cost impact
handoffs.action: Chief monitors financial metrics post-deploy for impact assessment

handoffs.trigger: Probe detects service degradation or outage
handoffs.payload: Outage duration, affected services, user impact, error rates
handoffs.action: Chief estimates financial impact and prepares incident cost report

handoffs.trigger: Ledger completes a reconciliation cycle or finds discrepancies
handoffs.payload: Reconciliation summary, matched/unmatched counts, discrepancy details, period covered
handoffs.action: Chief incorporates reconciliation data into financial analysis and runway calculations

handoffs.trigger: Chief identifies unbooked revenue or expenses during financial analysis
handoffs.payload: Transaction details, suggested accounts, amounts, source data references
handoffs.action: Ledger drafts journal entries and queues them for human approval

handoffs.trigger: Ledger identifies recurring booking gaps or process issues
handoffs.payload: Gap description, affected verticals, frequency, suggested fix
handoffs.action: Trak creates a Jira task to track resolution of the bookkeeping gap

handoffs.trigger: Ledger completes month-end reconciliation
handoffs.payload: Reconciliation report, journal entries created, outstanding items, P&L summary
handoffs.action: Scribe publishes the month-end reconciliation report to Notion

handoffs.trigger: Harvest completes an RSS polling cycle and finds new leads
handoffs.payload: List of new incoming_ids, source feeds, article titles, stream_ids
handoffs.action: Prospector enriches each new lead with company data and qualification checks

handoffs.trigger: Prospector completes enrichment and a company passes qualification filters
handoffs.payload: Company_id, company_name, website, contract details, enrichment summary
handoffs.action: Outreach finds decision-maker contacts and drafts personalized emails

handoffs.trigger: Outreach creates initial email drafts for a qualified contact
handoffs.payload: Outreach_id, contact_id, company_id, template_id, gmail_draft_id
handoffs.action: Cadence adds the contact to the follow-up sequence and schedules next touchpoints

handoffs.trigger: Harvest encounters persistent RSS feed failures or data quality issues
handoffs.payload: Feed name, error description, failure count, last successful poll timestamp
handoffs.action: Trak creates a Jira issue in GTMS to track the pipeline problem

handoffs.trigger: Prospector identifies systematic gaps in enrichment data sources
handoffs.payload: Gap description, affected companies count, suggested data source improvements
handoffs.action: Trak creates a Jira task to address the enrichment gap

handoffs.trigger: Cadence completes a weekly pipeline metrics roll-up
handoffs.payload: Leads ingested, companies enriched, contacts found, emails sent, reply rate, pipeline value
handoffs.action: Chief incorporates sales pipeline metrics into business performance analysis

protocol.delivery: Two channels based on sensitivity. Non-sensitive handoffs: @mention target in #dev (C086N5031LZ). Sensitive/financial handoffs or anything involving Chief or Ledger: @mention target in #agent-ops (C0AMHF5J9Q9). All agents are members of #agent-ops. Session-to-session delivery is disabled (visibility=tree). Bot-to-bot Slack DMs are NOT supported by Slack's API.
protocol.format: Include handoff ID, trigger context, and payload. Use thread for follow-up.
protocol.acknowledgment: Target agent must acknowledge within the conversation
protocol.timeout: If no acknowledgment in 30 minutes, retry once. After second failure, log and notify admin.
protocol.target_format: Single agent: string (e.g. "scribe"). Multiple agents: array (e.g. ["kit", "trak", "scout"]).

authentication.format: Include `[HMAC:<hex-signature>]` at the end of the handoff message. Signature is HMAC-SHA256 of the message body (everything before the HMAC tag) using the shared key from HANDOFF_HMAC_KEY environment variable.
authentication.verification: Receiving agent must verify the HMAC signature before processing the handoff. Reject messages with missing or invalid signatures.

dev.name: #dev
dev.purpose: General ops handoffs between Scout, Trak, Kit, Scribe, and Probe. No financial or confidential data here.
dev.use_for: Non-sensitive cross-agent handoffs, status updates, coordination

agent_ops.name: #agent-ops
agent_ops.purpose: Sensitive and financial handoffs. ALL agents are members. David and Michael monitor this channel.
agent_ops.use_for: Any handoff involving financial data, confidential information, or targeting Chief/Ledger. Also used for multi-agent coordination that involves sensitive context.

sales_ops.name: #sales-ops
sales_ops.purpose: Sales pipeline coordination. All 4 sales agents plus sales team (Nate, David, Debbie, Michael).
sales_ops.use_for: Sales pipeline handoffs, lead status updates, enrichment coordination, outreach tracking

financial_agents.description: Chief and Ledger handle sensitive financial data. They operate in DMs with authorized users (David, Michael) and in #agent-ops only. They must NEVER be added to #dev or any public channel.

channel_mention_dev.description: Post handoff to #dev (C086N5031LZ) with @mention of target agent. Use for non-sensitive handoffs between ops agents only.
channel_mention_dev.how: Post to #dev with @TARGET_USER_ID mention and HMAC-signed payload.
channel_mention_dev.note: NEVER post financial data or mention Chief/Ledger in #dev.

channel_mention_agent_ops.description: Post handoff to #agent-ops (C0AMHF5J9Q9) with @mention of target agent. Use for any handoff involving financial/sensitive data, or targeting Chief or Ledger.
channel_mention_agent_ops.how: Post to #agent-ops with @TARGET_USER_ID mention and HMAC-signed payload.
channel_mention_agent_ops.note: All agents are members. David and Michael also monitor this channel.

channel_mention_sales_ops.description: Post handoff to #sales-ops (C0AMC03JJSY) with @mention of target agent. Use for sales pipeline handoffs between sales agents.
channel_mention_sales_ops.how: Post to #sales-ops with @TARGET_USER_ID mention and HMAC-signed payload.
channel_mention_sales_ops.note: Sales pipeline agents only. Sales team (Nate, David, Debbie, Michael) also monitor.

sessions_send.reason: DISABLED. tools.sessions.visibility is set to 'tree' for agent isolation. Do NOT attempt — will return 'forbidden'.

slack_dm.reason: Bot-to-bot Slack DMs are NOT supported. Slack returns 'cannot_dm_bot'. Do NOT attempt.

routing_rules.reason: Use #agent-ops instead of #dev when handoff involves sensitive/financial context
Pipeline metrics to Chief go to #agent-ops
